stories.title: Extreme Heat Threat at 2026 World Cup
stories.desc: Researchers warn that extreme heat could pose serious threats to players and fans at the 2026 FIFA World Cup starting in June, with rising temperatures creating safety concerns.

stories.title: Panini Launches Record-Breaking 2026 World Cup Album
stories.desc: Panini launched its largest-ever FIFA World Cup sticker album on April 28 at Wembley Stadium, featuring 980 stickers across 112 pages to accommodate all 48 teams.

stories.title: FIFA Confirms Iran Will Play in USA Despite Tensions
stories.desc: FIFA President Gianni Infantino confirmed during FIFA Congress in Vancouver that Iran will participate in the 2026 World Cup in the USA, rejecting speculation of replacement.

stories.title: Coca-Cola Partners with Panini for Exclusive World Cup Stickers
stories.desc: Coca-Cola teams with Panini to offer 12 exclusive World Cup stickers hidden in specially marked 20-oz bottles, featuring stars like Harry Kane and Lamine Yamal starting mid-May.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia and Senegal Schedule World Cup Friendly in San Antonio
stories.desc: Saudi Arabia and Senegal will play an international friendly on June 9 at Toyota Field in San Antonio as a warmup before the 2026 FIFA World Cup officially kicks off June 11.

stories.title: World Cup 2026 Drives Uneven Tourism Demand Across Host Nations
stories.desc: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is reshaping tourism across the US, Canada, and Mexico with rising demand, soaring hotel prices, and connectivity gaps as destinations race to attract visitors.
stories.source: Travel And Tour World 1 day ago
